skill.name: SEO Authority Builder
skill.description: Analyzes content for E-E-A-T signals and suggests improvements to build authority and trust.
skill.summary: Build search authority by optimizing Experience, Expertise, Authority, and Trust signals in your content.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill with no executable code, network access, or file system operations. The skill provides E-E-A-T analysis framework and SEO authority building guidance through structured prompts. No security risks detected - safe for publication.

content.user_title: Build Search Authority with E-E-A-T Optimization
content.value_statement: Search engines prioritize content with strong Experience, Expertise, Authority, and Trust signals. This skill audits your content and provides actionable steps to improve credibility and search rankings.
actual_capabilities: Audit content for E-E-A-T signal strength with scored assessments
Generate author bio templates with credential highlighting
Create trust signal checklists for YMYL content
Develop topical authority maps and content cluster strategies
Provide platform-specific optimization guidance for WordPress and static sites
limitations: Requires manual implementation of recommended changes on your website or CMS
Cannot directly access or analyze live websites without content being provided
SEO recommendations are guidance-based and do not guarantee ranking improvements
Does not replace professional SEO audits for large enterprise sites

use_cases.title: YMYL Content Optimization
use_cases.description: Analyze health, finance, or legal content to ensure it meets Google's strict E-E-A-T standards before publication.

use_cases.title: Author Credibility Enhancement
use_cases.description: Develop comprehensive author pages with credentials, expertise indicators, and trust signals that search engines recognize.
use_cases.target_user: Bloggers and content creators building personal brands

use_cases.title: Topical Authority Development
use_cases.description: Create content cluster strategies that establish your site as an authoritative source in your niche.

prompt_templates.title: Quick E-E-A-T Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this content for E-E-A-T signals. Rate each category from 1-10 and list the top 3 missing elements: [paste content]

prompt_templates.title: Author Bio Generator
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an authoritative author bio that highlights expertise and credentials for [profession/industry]. Include: qualifications, experience, notable achievements, and trust indicators.

prompt_templates.title: Trust Signal Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a comprehensive trust signal checklist for a [type of website] site. Include technical, content, and business credibility elements that improve E-E-A-T.

prompt_templates.title: Topical Authority Map
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a topical authority content strategy for [niche/topic]. Identify core pillar content, supporting cluster topics, internal linking structure, and semantic keyword relationships.

output_examples.input: E-E-A-T audit request for a financial advice article
output_examples.output:
E-E-A-T Score: 6/10

Strengths:
- Clear author attribution present
- Cites reputable financial institutions
- Includes disclaimer

Missing Elements:
1. Author credentials not displayed (add CPA or CFA designation)
2. No editorial review process mentioned
3. Missing last updated date for time-sensitive data
4. No links to primary research sources

Priority Actions:
- Add author bio with financial credentials
- Include editorial policy link
- Display content freshness indicators

output_examples.input: Author bio creation for a health blog
output_examples.output:
Author Bio Template:

[Name] is a [Credential, e.g., Registered Dietitian Nutritionist] with [X] years of experience in [specialty]. [He/She/They] holds [degree] from [University] and is a member of [Professional Organization]. [Name]'s work has been featured in [Publications] and [He/She/They] regularly [speaks/writes] on [topic]. All content is reviewed by [Medical Reviewer Name, MD] for accuracy.

best_practices: Always display author credentials prominently on YMYL content pages
Include editorial review dates and update policies to show content freshness
Link to authoritative external sources and cite primary research when making claims
anti_patterns: Publishing health or financial advice without qualified author credentials
Using anonymous or generic author bylines on important content
Failing to disclose conflicts of interest or affiliate relationships

faq.question: What is E-E-A-T and why does it matter for SEO?
faq.answer: E-E-A-T stands for Experience, Expertise, Authority, and Trust. Google uses these signals in its Quality Rater Guidelines to assess content quality. Strong E-E-A-T is especially critical for YMYL (Your Money Your Life) topics like health, finance, and legal content.

faq.question: How do I improve E-E-A-T for an existing website?
faq.answer: Start by adding detailed author bios with credentials, creating an About page with team information, implementing editorial policies, adding trust signals like contact information and security badges, and building topical depth through comprehensive content clusters.

faq.question: What are trust signals and what should I include?
faq.answer: Trust signals are elements that demonstrate credibility to users and search engines. Include: SSL certificates, contact information, privacy policy, terms of service, author credentials, editorial guidelines, customer reviews, security badges, and social proof.

faq.question: Does this skill work for all types of websites?
faq.answer: Yes, the E-E-A-T framework applies to all content types. However, it is most critical for YMYL topics (health, finance, legal) where Google applies stricter quality standards. E-commerce, news, and informational sites all benefit from E-E-A-T optimization.

faq.question: Can this skill analyze my live website?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides analysis based on content you provide. Copy and paste your content, describe your site structure, or share specific pages you want optimized. For comprehensive site audits, consider combining with crawling tools.

faq.question: What schema markup should I implement for E-E-A-T?
faq.answer: Key schemas include: Person or Organization for author/entity information, Article with author and datePublished properties, Review for testimonials, FAQPage for FAQ content, and BreadcrumbList for navigation. Use Google's Structured Data Testing Tool to validate.
